rulings.Case Title: JOHN KETOLA VS. PLANT CONSTRUCTION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 28, 2011, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JOHN KETOLA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement IS Denied without prejudice to re-filing if the re-filed papers include a settlement agreement signed by the defendant. Neither party provides a copy of the settlement agreement that defendant executed and CCP 664.6 requires direct party participation. (Levy v. Superior Court, (1995) 10 Cal.4th 578, 585). In addition, the motion appears unnecessary and the parties are encouraged to continue to meet and confer regarding any remaining issues that may be holding up payment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: BRADLEY R. WHITE, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE et al VS. ALBERT K. CLIFFORD AKA AL CLIFFORD, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For An Order Compelling Deft'S To Appear For Their Depositions And Produce Documents And Mtn For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft'S And Their Attorneys
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 28, 2011, Line 1, PLAINTIFFa BRADLEY R. WHITE, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE, CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY's Notice Of Motion For An Order Compelling Defendants To Appear For Their Depositions And Produce Documents And Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorneys. Because Judge Kahn had numerous ex parte conversations about other cases involving Ms. Sagonowsky in Judge Kahn¿s role as a settlement judge, including several ex parte conversations with Ms. Sagonowsky and Mr. White, Judge Kahn is recusing himself. However, Judge Kahn has read the papers on this motion and is willing to serve as a ¿mediator¿ to assist the parties in resolving their disputes regarding scheduling depositions. The parties are directed to meet and confer to determine if all of them wish Judge Kahn¿s assistance as a mediator. If they do, they should appear at 9am. If they do not or, if they do and are still not able to resolve all the issues raised by this motion, the motion is continued to December 19, 2011 at 9am and will be heard by a judge other than Judge Kahn. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRADLEY R. WHITE, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE et al VS. ALBERT K. CLIFFORD AKA AL CLIFFORD, AN INDIVIDUAL et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 28, 2011, Line 1, DEFENDANTs MARIA MORGAN-BUTCHER, ALBERT CLIFFORD, GORDON CLIFFORD REALTY, INC. Motion To Compel The Continued Deposition Of Plaintiffs. Because Judge Kahn had numerous ex parte conversations about other cases involving Ms. Sagonowsky in Judge Kahn¿s role as a settlement judge, including several ex parte conversations with Ms. Sagonowsky and Mr. White, Judge Kahn is recusing himself. However, Judge Kahn has read the papers on this motion and is willing to serve as a ¿mediator¿ to assist the parties in resolving their disputes regarding scheduling depositions. The parties are directed to meet and confer to determine if all of them wish Judge Kahn¿s assistance as a mediator. If they do, they should appear at 9am. If they do not or, if they do and are still not able to resolve all the issues raised by this motion, the motion is continued to December 19, 2011 at 9am and will be heard by a judge other than Judge Kahn. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HELLER EHRMAN LLP VS. HEADTHERE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 28, 2011. Line 20. DEFENDANT ROY SANDBERG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. Defendant¿s contention that case should be arbitrated needs to be raised by a motion to compel arbitration. Defendant can reasonably respond to the complaint and the economic litigation rules bar special demurrers in a limited jurisdiction case. Judge Kahn discloses that he was employed by Heller Ehrman as an associate from 1983 to 1987, but does not believe that his past association with Heller Ehrman affects his ability to fair and impartial in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
